title: carex rupestris subsp. altimontana
intro: Carex rupestris subsp. altimontana, also known as Mountain Sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex rupestris plant, which is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is primarily found in high-altitude mountainous regions and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is known to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Carex rupestris subsp. altimontana can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
